generated_oml:
instance HeatbedActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Resistive heating element converting electrical energy to thermal energy. Operates under bang-bang control with discrete on/off states governed by controller parameters: HeatingDuration (H) and WaitingDuration (C). Enforces safety protocols via low-level driver to prevent overheating with upper temperature limit (UL)."
]

instance FanActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Distributes heated air uniformly inside the styrofoam box to ensure uniform temperature distribution (validating Assumption 1). Operation synchronized with heatbed via controller."
]

instance RabbitMQCommunicationInterface : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Remote control interface enabling asynchronous message-based communication between Digital Twin and Physical Twin controller on Raspberry Pi. Facilitates command issuance (e.g., adjusting H or C parameters) and enforces safe protocol translation."
]

instance InternalTemperatureSensor1 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "First internal temperature sensor monitoring air temperature inside the styrofoam box with 0.5°C accuracy. Part of three-sensor array ensuring uniform temperature distribution."
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureData1
]

instance InternalTemperatureSensor2 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Second internal temperature sensor monitoring air temperature inside the styrofoam box with 0.5°C accuracy. Part of three-sensor array ensuring uniform temperature distribution."
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureData2
]

instance ExternalTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "External temperature sensor measuring ambient room temperature. Provides environmental context for thermal regulation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ExternalTemperatureData
]

instance InternalTemperatureData1 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor1
]

instance InternalTemperatureData2 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor2
]

instance ExternalTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ExternalTemperatureSensor
]

instance AggregatedTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor1, InternalTemperatureSensor2
    base:desc "Aggregated temperature data computed by averaging readings from two internal sensors to ensure uniform temperature representation."
]

instance ThermalModelA : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simplified thermal model assuming all electrical energy from heatbed converts to heat energy (Assumption 4). Derived from energy balance equation Q = cmΔT with four core assumptions: uniform temperature distribution, negligible wall heat accumulation, constant specific heat capacity, and complete energy conversion."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelCalibrationEnabler
]

instance ThermalModelB : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Advanced thermal model relaxing Assumption 4 to account for heatbed thermal inertia. Incorporates four free parameters for improved thermal dynamic prediction. Selected for superior predictive accuracy over Model A."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelCalibrationEnabler, KalmanFilterEnabler
]

instance RealTimeTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Continuous real-time temperature measurements from internal and external sensors. Used for model calibration and real-time visualization."
    DTDFVocab:fromData InternalTemperatureData1, InternalTemperatureData2, ExternalTemperatureData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TimeSeriesDatabaseEnabler, ModelCalibrationEnabler
]

instance HistoricalTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Archived temperature measurements stored in time-series database for trend analysis, what-if simulations, and model validation."
    DTDFVocab:fromData AggregatedTemperatureData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CoSimulationEnabler
]

instance ControllerParameterData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Configuration parameters for bang-bang controller: LowerTemperatureLimit (LL), UpperTemperatureLimit (UL), HeatingDuration (H), WaitingDuration (C). Used for simulation and optimization."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CoSimulationEnabler, ControllerOptimizationEnabler
]

instance ModelCalibrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Parameter estimation framework adjusting Model B parameters using real-world data to ensure alignment between Digital Twin and Physical Twin. Implements calibration process described in Section 3.3."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, AdaptiveCalibrationService
]

instance TimeSeriesDatabaseEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Implied time-series database storing historical and real-time temperature measurements. Enables visualization of temperature trends and supports data-driven decision making."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeVisualizationService
]

instance RabbitMQMessageBrokerEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Open-source message broker facilitating asynchronous communication between Physical Twin components (low-level driver, controller) and Digital Twin. Ensures decoupled, scalable data exchange for real-time operations."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataCollectionService, CommandExecutionService
]

instance KalmanFilterEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "State estimation algorithm correlating measured data with non-measured states (e.g., heatbed temperature). Used for anomaly detection and heatbed state estimation."
    DTDFVocab:enables AnomalyDetectionService, SelfAdaptationService
]

instance CoSimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Integration environment combining historical or real-time Physical Twin data with Digital Twin models for predictive analysis and controller parameter optimization."
    DTDFVocab:enables WhatIfSimulationService, ControllerOptimizationService
]

instance ControllerOptimizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Optimization framework refining controller parameters (LL, UL, H, C) based on simulation results to improve thermal regulation performance."
    DTDFVocab:enables ControllerTuningService
]

instance DataCollectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous acquisition of sensor data (internal/external temperatures) from Physical Twin via low-level driver and RabbitMQ message broker."
    DTDFVocab:provides TemperatureDataInsight
]

instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization of incubator status by relaying data to time-series database and plotting into dashboard. Enables dynamic observation of system behavior."
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemStatusInsight
]

instance AdaptiveCalibrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automated execution of model calibration process to update Model B parameters based on real-world data, ensuring alignment between Digital Twin and Physical Twin."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelAccuracyInsight
]

instance AnomalyDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Identification of deviations from expected behavior by correlating measured data with non-measured states (e.g., heatbed temperature) using Kalman Filter."
    DTDFVocab:provides AnomalyAlertAction
]

instance WhatIfSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Predictive analysis of system responses under hypothetical scenarios by integrating historical or real-time Physical Twin data into simulations."
    DTDFVocab:provides PredictionInsight
]

instance ControllerOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Refinement of controller parameters (LL, UL, H, C) based on simulation results to achieve optimal thermal regulation."
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimizationRecommendationAction
]

instance SelfAdaptationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Closed-loop adaptation process triggered by behavioral changes in Physical Twin (e.g., introduction of cold objects). Includes anomaly detection, data collection, model recalibration, and controller retuning."
    DTDFVocab:provides AdaptationStatusInsight, ControllerRetuningAction
]

instance RealTimeVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Dashboard integration displaying incubator status including temperature trends, controller states, and system performance metrics."
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualizationInsight
]

instance CommandExecutionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Issuance of control commands (e.g., adjusting H or C parameters) from Digital Twin to Physical Twin controller via RabbitMQ interface."
    DTDFVocab:provides CommandExecutionStatusAction
]

instance ControllerTuningService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service for tuning controller parameters based on optimization results and simulation feedback."
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimizationRecommendationAction
]

instance TemperatureDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Continuous stream of temperature measurements from internal and external sensors providing real-time environmental context."
]

instance SystemStatusInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Current operational state of the incubator including temperature trends, controller activation status, and system performance metrics."
]

instance ModelAccuracyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantitative measure of alignment between Digital Twin model predictions and Physical Twin behavior after calibration."
]

instance AnomalyAlertAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated alert triggered when Kalman Filter detects deviations from expected system behavior."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance PredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Forecasted system responses under hypothetical scenarios including temperature trajectories and controller performance metrics."
]

instance OptimizationRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Suggested adjustments to controller parameters (LL, UL, H, C) based on simulation results to improve thermal regulation."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance AdaptationStatusInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Current status of self-adaptation process including detected behavioral changes, calibration progress, and system reconfiguration steps."
]

instance ControllerRetuningAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated reconfiguration of controller parameters and Kalman Filter based on updated system dynamics after adaptation."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance VisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Graphical representation of temperature trends, controller states, and system performance metrics enabling intuitive monitoring."
]

instance CommandExecutionStatusAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Confirmation of successful command execution including parameter adjustments and resulting system state changes."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance IncubatorEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Physical environment containing the styrofoam box, heatbed, fan, and temperature sensors. Operates within thermal regulation domain with controlled internal temperature regardless of external conditions."
    base:contains HeatbedActuator, FanActuator, InternalTemperatureSensor1, InternalTemperatureSensor2, ExternalTemperatureSensor
]

instance DigitalTwinFramework : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Centralized Digital Twin framework hosting simulation models, calibration algorithms, and decision support services. Operates as monolithic architecture with clear scope separation between data collection, model execution, and control optimization."
    base:contains ThermalModelA, ThermalModelB, RealTimeTemperatureData, HistoricalTemperatureData, ControllerParameterData,
                 ModelCalibrationEnabler, TimeSeriesDatabaseEnabler, RabbitMQMessageBrokerEnabler,
                 KalmanFilterEnabler, CoSimulationEnabler, ControllerOptimizationEnabler
]

instance CommunicationInfrastructure : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Asynchronous message-passing system facilitating data exchange between Physical Twin and Digital Twin components. Implements safe protocols and decoupled architecture for real-time operations."
    base:contains RabbitMQCommunicationInterface, RabbitMQMessageBrokerEnabler
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in the incubator DT system involves **control commands** transmitted from the DT (or its components) to the PT via the **RabbitMQ server**. The **controller** (implemented on a Raspberry Pi) listens for commands to **activate/deactivate the heatbed and fan**, using a **bang-bang control strategy** with parameters **LL (Lower Limit), UL (Upper Limit), H (Heating Duration), and C (Waiting Duration)** (Document 4). Commands are validated implicitly via the **safe protocol** embedded in the low-level driver, which ensures the PT remains in a safe state (e.g., preventing overheating). **Feedback** is provided via the **periodic temperature sensor data** streamed back to the DT, enabling closed-loop control. No explicit **authentication mechanisms**, **conflict resolution**, or **command execution confirmation** protocols are detailed beyond the safe protocol. The **message format** is unspecified, but the system relies on RabbitMQ’s message broker capabilities for reliable delivery."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system for the incubator employs a **periodic data acquisition cycle** where the low-level driver reads temperature data from the Physical Twin (PT) at unspecified but regular intervals (exact frequency not documented). The controller operates using a **bang-bang control strategy** with explicit timing parameters: **Heating duration (H)** and **Waiting duration (C)**, which define the temporal granularity of control actions. The system does not specify synchronization frequencies between the PT and DT beyond the periodic sensor readings and controller-driven actuation cycles. No latency requirements or real-time constraints are explicitly defined in the provided documents. Future goals (not yet implemented) include **on-demand calibration** and **Kalman filter-based anomaly detection**, which would introduce additional temporal dynamics but are not operational in the current system description. The temporal granularity for simulations (e.g., what-if scenarios) is also unspecified in the documents."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin constellation for the incubator follows a **cyber-physical system (CPS) architecture** centered on **RabbitMQ** as the core communication backbone. The system integrates three key layers: **(1) Physical Layer**: the PT (box, heatbed, fan, sensors); **(2) Cyber Layer**: the low-level driver, controller, and simulation models (Model A/B); and **(3) Data Layer**: RabbitMQ for message brokering. The **orchestration** is driven by the **controller’s bang-bang logic**, which relies on **periodic sensor data** from the low-level driver. **Calibration algorithms** (described in section 3.3) are used to update Model A/B parameters (e.g., *C_air*, *G_box*, *C_heater*, *G_heater*) based on experimental data. The **resource management** is implicit, with no explicit orchestration engine; instead, the system leverages **message-driven coordination** via RabbitMQ. The **component integration** is tightly coupled: the low-level driver feeds sensor data to the controller, which adjusts the heatbed/fan via RabbitMQ. The **system-wide coordination** is achieved through **asynchronous messaging**, with no distributed consensus or fault-tolerance mechanisms described. Future goals (e.g., self-adaptation loop) are not yet implemented but would require extending this architecture with **dynamic calibration triggers** and **Kalman filter-based anomaly detection** (not currently deployed)."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle for the incubator case study includes the following phases based on the provided documents:

1. **Development Phase**:
   - **Hardware Setup**: The Physical Twin (PT) consists of an insulated styrofoam box, a heatbed, a fan, two internal temperature sensors, and one external temperature sensor (Document 2, Section 2.1).
   - **Software Development**: The software stack includes a low-level driver for sensor/actuator communication, a controller (bang-bang control logic with parameters LL, UL, H, C), and a RabbitMQ-based communication server for message brokering (Document 5, Section 2.2).
   - **Modeling Phase**: Two dynamic models (Model A and Model B) were developed to predict the temperature inside the box, with Model B incorporating a more accurate representation of the heatbed's thermal inertia (Document 6, Section 3). Model A relies on all assumptions (e.g., uniform temperature distribution, negligible heat accumulation in walls), while Model B relaxes Assumption 4 (ignoring heatbed energy absorption).
   - **Calibration**: Models are calibrated using experimental data to refine parameters (Document 1, Figure 1; Document 4, Section 3.3).

2. **Deployment Phase**:
   - The DT integrates models, real-time data from sensors, and algorithms (e.g., calibration, monitoring) to replicate and predict the PT's behavior (Document 1).
   - The PT and DT communicate via RabbitMQ, enabling real-time data exchange between the controller, low-level driver, and external DT components (Document 5, Figure 7).

3. **Operation Phase (Implied but Not Detailed in Documents)**:
   - The documents describe the DT's potential to enable real-time visualization, simulations, and anomaly detection (Document 4, Section 5), but these are framed as future work and not implemented in the provided snippets. No explicit lifecycle governance (e.g., version control, transition management) or phases beyond development/deployment are documented.

**Note**: The documents do not provide details on lifecycle stages beyond development and deployment, nor do they describe phases like 'Evolution' or 'Operation' with specific technical implementations (e.g., Kalman filters, self-adaptation loops)."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
**Model Fidelity**:
- **Model A** (highest fidelity under Assumptions 1–4) is less accurate for heatbed thermal inertia due to its strict energy-conversion assumption.
- **Model B** (relaxes Assumption 4) better predicts heatbed dynamics but requires more complex calibration.
- **Validation Methods**: Experimental validation (Section 4) compares model predictions (e.g., Model B’s calibration results in Figure 13) against real-time sensor data, confirming Model B’s superiority for thermal inertia.

**Uncertainty Sources**:
- **Assumption 3** (constant specific heat capacity) may introduce errors under varying humidity/pressure.
- **Assumption 4** (ignored heatbed absorption) limits Model A’s accuracy for transient heatbed behavior.
- **Sensor Accuracy**: Internal/external sensors (0.5°C resolution) may contribute to measurement uncertainty.

**Error Detection and Correction**:
- **Calibration Algorithms** (Section 3.3) adjust model parameters (e.g., **G_box**) using real-time data to minimize prediction errors.
- **Experimental Validation**: Section 4’s tests (e.g., uniform temperature distribution) verify model assumptions and correct deviations.
- **Quality Assurance**: No explicit QA processes are described, but the open-source repository ([2]) implies reproducibility and peer verification.

**Note**: Claims about Kalman filters, anomaly detection, or self-adaptation loops (Section 5) are **not in the provided documents** and thus excluded.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **technical network infrastructure** for the incubator DT is centered around **RabbitMQ**, an open-source message broker deployed on the **Raspberry Pi** (Document 4). The **network topology** follows a **pub-sub model**, where the low-level driver and controller publish messages to RabbitMQ, and DT components (e.g., monitors, Kalman filters) subscribe to these messages. The **communication protocol** is **AMQP (Advanced Message Queuing Protocol)**, leveraging RabbitMQ’s lightweight, scalable design. No details are provided on **bandwidth/latency requirements**, **redundancy mechanisms** (e.g., clustering), or **security protocols** (e.g., TLS, authentication). The system assumes **local, on-premise deployment** with minimal network overhead, as the PT and DT components are co-located on the Raspberry Pi."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The **hosting and deployment infrastructure** for the incubator DT is **edge-based**, with all components running on a **Raspberry Pi 4 Model B** (Document 2). The **software stack** includes:
  - **RabbitMQ** for message brokering,
  - **Low-level driver** for sensor/actuator interaction,
  - **Controller** (bang-bang logic) for temperature regulation,
  - **Simulation models (Model A/B)** for predictive analytics.
  The system does not require **cloud hosting**, **GPU acceleration**, or **containerization** (e.g., Docker). **Scalability** is limited to the Raspberry Pi’s capabilities, with no mention of distributed deployment. **Availability/reliability** is ensured by the **safe protocol** in the low-level driver and the **periodic data synchronization** via RabbitMQ. The **hardware and software specifications** are publicly available in the **GitHub repository** ([Document 2, 7]), but no details on **high-availability mechanisms** (e.g., failover, redundancy) are provided."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The incubator Digital Twin system exhibits **minimal horizontal integration** with external systems. The primary **data exchange protocol** is **RabbitMQ’s native AMQP (Advanced Message Queuing Protocol)**, used for internal communication between the low-level driver, controller, and DT components. There is **no explicit integration with external APIs, cloud services, or enterprise systems** described in the documents. Dependencies are limited to: **(1) RabbitMQ** (open-source message broker) for internal synchronization; **(2) Python’s Scipy package** (for optimization during calibration); and **(3) the PT’s hardware components** (sensors, heatbed, fan). The system does not rely on **third-party Digital Twin platforms**, **IoT gateways**, or **cloud-based DT services**. The only **interoperability standard** implicitly used is **AMQP via RabbitMQ**, but this is confined to internal operations. The documents do not mention **data exchange formats**, **API gateways**, or **cross-DT integration patterns**, indicating a **closed-loop, self-contained architecture** with no external dependencies beyond basic hardware and software libraries."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system for the incubator incorporates **safety-critical mechanisms** primarily through the **low-level driver**, which includes a **safe protocol** to maintain the device in a safe operational state. The **controller** implements a **bang-bang control strategy** with predefined safety parameters: **LL (Lower Limit for temperature)**, **UL (Upper Limit for temperature)**, **H (Heating duration)**, and **C (Waiting duration)**. These parameters ensure the system adheres to safe temperature bounds (LL and UL) and prevents overheating by regulating heating and cooling cycles. The **communication server (RabbitMQ)** facilitates synchronized data exchange between the controller and low-level driver, ensuring real-time monitoring and command execution without compromising system safety. No explicit mention of cybersecurity protocols (e.g., authentication, encryption), access control, or compliance frameworks (e.g., GDPR, ISO 27001) is provided in the documents."
]
